description: A White House-designed two-panel statistics infographic split by a diagonal grey divider. The top panel shows President Trump standing in a White House interior (dark navy suit, light blue tie, flag lapel pin) beside a gilded eagle finial and holiday greenery; overlaid white text reads '2.5 MILLION' (large) then 'Native Born Americans Gained Employment' with a small 'Over the past year'. The bottom panel shows Trump walking on the South Lawn grass, pointing, with vehicles and trees behind; overlaid is a red box with white text '670,000' and white text 'Foreign Born Workers Lost Employment' plus 'Over the past year'. The engraved 'The WHITE HOUSE' logo sits top-right and a small 'BLS' (Bureau of Labor Statistics) source credit is bottom-right. Visual style: polished photo-composite stat card with bold contrasting numbers (positive figure in white/neutral, negative figure in alarm-red). Propaganda technique: a juxtaposition/contrast argument crediting the administration with boosting native-born employment while foreign-born employment fell, advancing an 'Americans First' jobs narrative. Target audience: the base and persuadable voters concerned about jobs and immigration.
notable_text: 2.5 MILLION Native Born Americans Gained Employment Over the past year; 670,000 Foreign Born Workers Lost Employment Over the past year; The WHITE HOUSE; BLS
